stimulus: With its conventional storefronts, parking signs, and overcast sky, Stephen Shore's 1973 photograph Beverly Boulevard and La Brea Avenue, Los Angeles may strike viewers as offering little reward to anyone hunting for visual depth. ______blank art historian Britt Salvesen has built an entire chapter of her book around this single image, arguing that its careful geometry redefines what a vernacular American landscape can be.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Even so" logically signals contrast: although the photograph looks unpromising for visual analysis, Salvesen nonetheless finds it richly rewarding. The second sentence overturns the expectation set up in the first.

Choice A is incorrect because "to put it another way" would mark the second sentence as a paraphrase of the first, but it contradicts rather than restates it. Choice B is incorrect because "accordingly" would signal cause and effect, but Salvesen's deep engagement is not a consequence of the image's apparent dullness. Choice D is incorrect because "similarly" would suggest the two sentences make the same point, but the second sentence contrasts with the first.
